title: From Ground to Sky: Mizoram Gears Up to Host First-Ever NCC Fest & Parasailing Camp
content:
566/2025-2026

Aizawl, 31st March 2026 :In a major initiative aimed at youth empowerment and nation-building, Colonel Sagar K Mohanty, Commanding Officer of 2 Mizo Battalion NCC, called on the Hon'ble Chief Minister of Mizoram, Pu Lalduhoma, yesterday, to apprise him of the recent developments and expansion of NCC activities in South Mizoram.

During the interaction, Col Mohanty highlighted the organisation of the first-ever NCC Fest and Parasailing Camp in Mizoram, to be held at Lunglei and scheduled from 05 May to 11 May, a pioneering initiative designed to spread awareness about NCC and motivate the youth to join its ranks. The event aims to instill discipline, confidence, and a spirit of adventure among young individuals, guiding them towards becoming responsible citizens and active contributors to nation-building.

The upcoming fest will feature interactive sessions with distinguished dignitaries of the state and senior military officials, providing cadets and youth with valuable exposure and inspiration. A wide array of attractions will be set up, including informative NCC stalls, weapon displays by Border Security Force, Assam Rifles & Territorial Army Bn, NCC alumni interactions, and exhibitions showcasing local handloom and traditional crafts. Additionally, traditional food stalls will highlight the rich cultural heritage of Mizoram, making the event both educational and culturally enriching.

The Hon'ble Chief Minister appreciated the visionary efforts of Col. Sagar K Mohanty and commended the initiative as a significant step towards the holistic development and upliftment of Mizoram's youth. He emphasised the vital role of NCC in shaping character, leadership and unity among the younger generation. He also conveyed his consent to grace the occasion at Lunglei, reflecting his strong encouragement for such initiatives.

This historic initiative marks a new chapter in NCC activities in Mizoram, creating a vibrant platform to connect, inspire and empower youth through innovation, adventure and cultural pride.

Attachment

title: Mizorama NCC Fest & Parasailing Camp hmasa ber tur atan inbuatsaihna kalpui mek a ni
content:
5056/2025-2026

Aizawl, 31 March 2026 :Nimin khan Mizoram Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chu a pisaah 2 Mizo Battalion NCC Commanding Officer Colonel Sagar K Mohanty chuan hmuin, Mizoram chhim lama NCC ṭhan chhoh dan leh ṭhalaite hmasawnna tura hma lakna hrang hrangte a hrilhfiah.

Col. Sagar K Mohanty chuan Mizorama NCC Fest & Parasailing Camp hmasa ber tur, May ni 5–11 chhunga Lungleia buatsaih tum a nih thu sawiin, NCC chungchang hriat zauna tur leh ṭhalaite'n NCC zawm tura fuih phurna tura ruahman a nih thu a sawi a. Khua leh tui ṭha ni tura ṭhalaite chher hriam leh mahni inthunun theihna, inrin tawkna leh huaisenna rilru pu tura kaihhruai chu he hma lakna hian a tum a nih thu a sawi.

Chief Minister chuan Col. Sagar K Mohanty hma lakna chu fakin, Mizoram ṭhalaite hmasawnna atana hma lakna pawimawh tak a nih thu a sawi a. NCC chu ṭhalaite nungchang siamtu leh hruaitu nihna kawnga chher hriamtu pawimawh tak a nih thu sawiin, Lungleia NCC Fest hmanpui tura sawmna pawh chu tihhlawhtlin ngei a tum thu leh an hma lakna a phurpui thu a sawi.

NCC Fest-ah hian sawrkar hotu pawimawh leh sipai hotu liante nena inkawmna hun (interactive session) te, NCC chhuak (alumni)-te nena inkawmna hun te a awm dawn a. NCC thil leh hriat tur pawimawh pho chhuahna leh Border Security Force, Assam Rifles leh Territorial Army Bn te ralthuam pho chhuahna te buatsaih a nih dawn bakah, Mizoram tualchhuak puan leh themthiam thil mawi tak tak pho chhuah a ni dawn bawk a. Mizo ei tur chi hrang hrang te pawh chhawp chhuah a ni ang.

Attachment

title: KOLASIB DISTRICT MAGISTRATE-IN RAMRI HRULA FIMKHUR TURIN MIPUITE A NGEN
content:
5055/2025-2026

Kolasib, 31st March, 2026 :Vawiin khan Kolasib District Magistrate, Pu Lalmuan Puia chuan thuchhuah siam in; tun hnai maia thenawm Assam state-in Inthlanpui (General Election) an hmachhawn dawn avangin, Election Commission of India (ECI) inkaihhruaina hnuaiah state inrina (Inter-State border) huam chhungah te venhimna uluk zawk leh khauh zawka kalpui mek a ni a, duty leh patrolling te pawh neih uar a ni, a ti a. Hemi a nih avang hian ramri hrula khawsa leh veivak thinte pawh fimkhur thiam tur leh, thil pawi thleng thei laka inveng theuh turin mipuite a ngen a ni.

Ni 29.03.2026 zana Bairabi Zophai-a boruak tha lo tak thleng kha Kolasib District Administration chuan pawi a ti hle a. Hetiang thil duhawm lo hi a thlen nawn tawh loh nan District Administration chuan a tul ang zelin hma a la chhunzawm dawn a ni, a ti.

District Magistrate chuan mipuite pawh thlaphang lova thlamuang taka awm tur leh District Administration leh Sorkar hmalaknate thlawp a, theihtawp chhuah tlang theuh turin mitinte a ngen bawk.

Attachment

title: ROPUILIANI WOMEN FARMER PRODUCER COMPANY LIMITED IN AIENG MT 71 AN LEISAK CHU VAWIIN KHAN KOLASIB-AH VAILIAM A NI
content:
5054/2025-2026

Kolasib, 31st March, 2026:MzSRLM hnuaia din Ropuiliani Women Farmer Producer Company Limited (FPC) chuan a shareholders te tharchhuah Aieng Metric Ton 71 (KG 71,600) Kolasib leh Mamit District atanga a leisak chu, vawiin khan Pu Lalmuanpuia, District Mission Director/Project Director, DRDO Kolasib District chuan Kolasib DC Office Complex atangin a vailiam.

He flag off programme hi Dr. Doris Lalrinawmi, Block Mission Director/Block Development Officer, BMMU, Thingdawl R.D Block chuan a kaihruai a. Khuallian Pu Lalmuanpuia chuan Mizoram State Rural Livelihoods Mission (MzSRLM) hnuaia agri-marketing value chain atana din Ropuiliani Women FPC in Kolasib leh Mamit District huamchhunga a shareholders te hnen atanga a leisak Aieng MT 71 zet, SSB AGRO LLP, Mizoram hnena hralhchhuah a ni chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a ni.

Ropuiliani Company chu kuthnathawktu Self Help Group (SHG) te tana innghahna tlak ni ngei tura Ministry of Rural Development-in a din a nih thu sawiin, MzSRLM chu he company bultantu an nih thu Pu Lalmuanpuia chuan a tarlang a. SHG te hi mi taima tan chuan chhungkaw eizawnna nghahchhan nghet a nih theih thu leh, sawrkar flagship programme-ah pawh sawhthing, aieng leh hmarcha te hi kuthnathawktute tharchhuah zara ramin hma a sawn theihna tur atana tum ber a nih thu a sawi bawk. Hengte hi hlawhtling thei turin Ropuiliani company chuan ruahmanna mumal tak a nei a, a thawktute pawhin a puitlin nan theihtawp chhuah turin a chah a ni.

Ropuiliani Women Farmer Producer Company Limited hian tun dinhmunah District 7 leh R.D Block 16 huam tel tawhin, shareholders (SHG) mi 9,500 chuang an inziaklut tawh a. An member-te hnen atang hian sawhthing leh Mizo hmarcha te lo leisak tawhin, tun dinhmunah Aieng leh sawhthing District dang atanga lei leh turin ruahmanna kalpui mek a ni. He company hian heng thlaite hi a hringa hralhchhuah piah lamah, Company Processing Unit, Chhingchhip-ah value addition kalpui mekin hmalak chhunzawm zel tum a ni.

He hunah hian Pu Henry Saingura, CEO, Ropuiliani Women FPC chuan company report tawi pein, Pu Samuel Lalmalsawma Sailo, Block Mission Director/Block Development Officer, BMMU, Bilkhawthlir R.D Block chuan lawmthu sawina hun hmangin hun a khar a ni.

Attachment
